legs.label: Draft the framing to react to
legs.description: Draft the first problem-framing from the handed-in material, for the director to react to.
legs.lead: Backstage hands Raven a strong first draft — never a blank question.
beats: Reads what the director handed in and frames the problem(s) underneath it, in service of facts and logic, not the solution.
Marks each piece of evidence honestly — first-hand, assumed, or hypothetical — and names the thin spot that still needs a real instance.
Hands Raven the draft plus the one thing to draw out, carrying the evidence bar and the Mom-Test follow-up.

legs.label: React to the draft — give feedback, or approve to finish
legs.description: The director reacts to the draft through Raven — feedback to fold in, or approval to finish.
legs.lead: The one switchback: the director is the checker, in his own voice.
beats: Raven puts the current draft in front of the director.
He reacts — gives feedback to fold in, or approves to finish.
Approval ends the run; any feedback loops back to a revise pass.

legs.label: Fold in the director's reaction
legs.description: Rewrite the framing whole to fold in the director's reaction, holding the evidence line.
legs.lead: Backstage folds his reaction in and rewrites the document, not a diff.
beats: Keeps what he confirmed, changes what he corrected, adds the evidence he gave — re-marked honestly.
Surfaces contradictions instead of silently picking, and holds that support for the solution needs a real instance.
Signals readiness when nothing real is still open; the draft returns to the director.
